title: Sunshine Protection Act of 2025
plain_summary: The Sunshine Protection Act of 2025 would make daylight saving time permanent all year long in the United States. This means people would never have to change their clocks forward or backward twice a year like they do now. All time zones would move forward by one hour and stay that way forever. States that do not currently follow daylight saving time can choose to keep their current time or switch to the new permanent time.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Sunshine Protection Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Making Daylight Savings Time Permanent
sections.text: This section makes daylight saving time permanent by changing how time zones work in the United States. First, it removes the part of an older law that made daylight saving time temporary. Then it changes the standard time for each time zone to be one hour ahead of what it used to be. This means the Eastern time zone will be three hours behind Greenwich Mean Time instead of four hours behind. The Central time zone will be four hours behind instead of five hours behind. This pattern continues for all time zones across the country. States or parts of states that had already chosen not to follow daylight saving time rules before this law passes can decide whether to use the new permanent time or keep their old time. The law also updates other related rules to make sure everything works together properly.
